pageTitle: All cities with insulation cost data | Woogoro
metaDescription: Insulation cost data by city and state. IECC climate zone, target R-value, and install method drive per-square-foot pricing more than house size alone.
ogDescription: Insulation pricing by city. IECC zone, R-value target, batt vs blown-in vs spray foam across metros.
schemaDescription: Directory of U.S. cities with 2026 insulation cost data, organized by state, including IECC zone and incentive-stack context per metro.
intro: Insulation cost is fundamentally a target-R-value question by IECC climate zone — Zone 7 (northern Maine, Minnesota, Mountain West) demands R-49+ in attics where Zone 2 (Florida, coastal TX) gets to R-30. Material choice layers on top: blown-in cellulose runs $1.50–$2.50 per square foot at R-30; fiberglass batts run $1–$2; closed-cell spray foam runs $4–$7 with the air-seal benefit baked in. Wall-vs-attic-vs-rim-joist matters a lot — wall retrofit through dense-pack drilling is 2–3x the linear cost of an open attic blow. The IRA 25C tax credit (30% up to $1,200) lands per project, so per-job effective price drops materially. The per-metro pages cover IECC climate zone, dominant install method, target R-value, and the IRA-25C plus state-rebate stack that lands per project.
